definition: Electronic Voting Machine, used to record votes electronically.
example: Voters press a button on the EVM next to their chosen candidate.

definition: A specific geographical area that elects a single representative.
example: India is divided into 543 parliamentary constituencies for Lok Sabha elections.

definition: The lower house of India's bicameral Parliament.
example: Members of the Lok Sabha are directly elected by the public.

definition: Guidelines issued by the ECI for conduct of political parties and candidates during elections.
example: The Model Code of Conduct prevents the ruling party from announcing new schemes right before an election.

definition: None of the Above, an option that allows voters to officially reject all candidates.
example: If a voter doesn't like any candidate, they can press the NOTA button.
